steps.label: The Analogy
steps.body: Object Storage is your warehouse. Block storage is your kitchen pantry. You don't cook from the warehouse — but you keep all the inventory there.

steps.label: How It Works
steps.body:
Akamai Object Storage is S3-compatible. Anything that speaks the S3 protocol (rclone, aws-cli, boto3, mc) works. You get:

- Per-cluster endpoints (E1, E2, E3 — different regions and capacity tiers)
- Bucket-level CORS, lifecycle, ACLs
- Bucket-level S3 access keys (scope per app)
- $5/month per 250 GB + $0.005/GB egress (often free up to a quota)

steps.label: Real World
steps.body: Use the E3 endpoint for high-throughput workloads. Migrate from AWS S3 with rclone copy s3:bucket linode:bucket --fast-list. Object Storage is the default origin behind Akamai CDN — pair them for global distribution.
"S3-compatible buckets at flat pricing — your warehouse for everything that doesn't need to live on a disk."

quiz.question: Your app uses boto3 against AWS S3. What's the migration story for Akamai Object Storage?

options.explain: There is no proprietary SDK. The S3 API is the API.

options.text: Change endpoint URL and access keys — code stays
options.explain: Object Storage is S3-compatible. You point boto3 at the cluster endpoint with new keys.
It's a config change, not a re-architecture.

options.text: Use only the linode-cli for uploads
options.explain: CLI works, but so does any S3 SDK.

quiz.question: Best tool to move 60TB from S3 to Akamai Object Storage?

options.text: aws s3 cp in a loop
options.explain: Single-threaded and slow. Not built for this scale.
rclone is purpose-built for inter-cloud migration. --fast-list batches LIST calls; --transfers tunes parallelism.

options.text: Manually drag and drop in the dashboard
Email Akamai support a hard drive
options.explain: There's no Snowball-style import service today.
